creator: jrhett@svcolo.com
text:
On Jan 9, 2008, at 7:50 PM, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
> Wed, Jan 09, 2008 at 11:12:21AM -0800, Jo Rhett wrote:
>> On Jan 9, 2008, at 10:53 AM, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
>>> May be.  But why are you telling that BDB >= 4.4 is needed?
>>> Do you have some negative experience?
>>
>> Well the configure script says explicitly
>>   AC_MSG_ERROR(This release of cfengine requires a version of  
>> BerkeleyDB
>> 4.4 or later)
>
> ;)) But even the configure from SVN trunk looks for 'db-4 db4
> db-4.6 db-4.5 db-4.4 db-4.3 db-4.2 db-4.1 db-4.0'.  So, the configure
> contradicts with itself.

I submitted a patch to fix that too, some time ago.

>>>>> Yes, it was patched.  And this was mentioned in the follow-up  
>>>>> to the PR.=
>>
>> I have the lastest trunk, and I don't see this.
>
> If 'this' means 'the patches that fix the issue with older BDB', then
>   http://svn.iu.hio.no/viewvc/trunk/src/instrument.c? 
> root=Cfengine-2&r1=401&r2=402
>   http://svn.iu.hio.no/viewvc/trunk/src/cfshow.c? 
> root=Cfengine-2&r1=418&r2=433

That's true.  Okay, asking the bug list for clarification.

>>> I am submitting patches both to the current port and to the  
>>> maintainer
>>> of the mainline code.  And I am trying to push both of them, because
>>> the port for the next version can be delayed (or the next version
>>> can be delayed by itself) and I prefer the ports to make their job.
>>
>> I disagree.  And this has been debated extensively on the ports  
>> list, and
>> consensus was that freebsd should not try to interfere with the  
>> maintainer.
>
> Didn't know about this.  A link to the discuission, please?

I don't have time to search the archives today, a bit rushed.  I'm  
sure you can search them yourself.  Try "ports patches policy bug  
fixes" or something like that.

>>    Also note that both 2.2.2 and 2.2.3 have come out since you  
>> reported
>> this, so frequency of updates to the mainline code base is not a  
>> problem.
>
> Hmm, I hope that the situation with this PR was an exception: it
> lied unanswered for 5 months and the port was not updated for the
> same time interval..

Yes, I complained about the inactive maintainer too ;-(

-- 
Jo Rhett
senior geek

Silicon Valley Colocation
Support Phone: 408-400-0550
